item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Across multiple sources, the image that was used in this article could not be sourced as cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). Thus, it is to be assumed that the image is non-compliant and has been removed.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Changing to just 'worn'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awww, I feel sad for him/her/it. Xe seems so scared and alone.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

I have been informed that the tale Worn is about SCP-1503, and think it provides a source for a wonderful crosslink. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-1503 is a sapient entity made entirely out of stone located on the southern face of Mount Ungaran in Central Java, Indonesia. SCP-1503's appearance is vaguely humanoid in form and shows signs of weathering and moss/lichen growth on its surface. Upon further inspection, SCP-1503's left index finger is significantly worn down when compared to its right index finger. Using time-lapse photography, it is determined that SCP-1503 is capable of moving at a maximum rate of 1m/yr.

To this:

SCP-1503 is a sapient entity made entirely out of stone located on the southern face of Mount Ungaran in Central Java, Indonesia. SCP-1503's appearance is vaguely humanoid in form and shows signs of weathering and moss/lichen growth on its surface. Upon further inspection, SCP-1503's left index finger is significantly worn down when compared to its right index finger. Using time-lapse photography, it is determined that SCP-1503 is capable of moving at a maximum rate of 1m/yr.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It hasn't been exploring and returning to the cave. It was in the cave from the second inscription to the last and has spent the last 656 years running directly away from the volcano. The last inscription was about nine hundred years ago.

Only the first two describe exploration, and can fit a timeline pretty easily (say the first walk was from 500 BCE to 100 CE), the second walk could have been right after (maybe 150 CE to 400 CE) and still leave plenty of time for the inscriptions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It feels like his stated rate of movement is just too slow for the times between inscriptions. That with how long it would take to make those with an available movement speed if 1m/year, it just wouldn't leave much time to actually go out and explore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And I thought this guy was slow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted the moment I read about trees trapping him and hurting his face.
Love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's what I was going for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's pretty good. Rather evocative. +1'ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do very much enjoy this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've deleted it. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can reword this. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can reword it.

As to your question…tracking it really. The fence is to mostly protect it from vandalism or theft, among other things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing that gets me is, if there really wasn't a lot of time left to run from this guy's perspective, why'd he spend the time to make the fifth note? Why not have him write it elsewhere?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The "ANALYSIS: INCONCLUSIVE" bit just does not need to be there. But otherwise, I like this quite a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. +1.

Small quibble in the last para: "…is believed to supersede all other inscriptions". "Supersede" implies that it makes them obsolete or wrong in some sense. I think the word you want is "postdate" all other inscriptions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to refrain from voting, I just don't understand this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Between the image and the vague, moody description, I do kinda like this one. It's not knock-you-flat brilliant or especially creative, but it has a sort of storytelling quality to it— like it's a thing that just exists without caring whether or not you find it interesting— that I like to see on the mainlist. A few nitpicks:

1) I have limited experience with translation (Latin, German) but the incomplete bits and suggested translations in the collapsible kinda bugged me— in a "language doesn't work quite like that" kind of way. Not a big deal, more of a nitpick.

2)

The area surrounding SCP-1503 within a 100m radius is to be deemed Site-75.

I don't think "deemed" is the right word. "Termed" is more scientific or "is known as", and I'd drop the "to be". I'm also wondering— are they more just tracking this thing than containing it? Since they just move the site as it moves?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am immediately reminded of this:

http://armorgames.com/play/14662/400-years

I guess the SCP isn't the only one who needs to get out more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I come back from a year away and see a neat little scip. I like this one.

This one is nice.

First +1 since I left.

(Don't let that go to your head, I'm nobody special around here. Just left for awhile.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unique and mysterious. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
made entirely out of stone

This feels a bit unspecific.

Otherwise, something about creatures having a wildly different perception of time fascinates me, and I don't think you were being too vague about it. The addition of a precise measure of the entity's speed really helps grasp the concept quickly, though. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, added. 1m/yr is its max speed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it moving? If yes, then at what speed? The article doesn't mention that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Too vague? The thing I was trying to emphasize here was its perception of time, where hundreds of years are perceived as mere moments. As well as the effects of weathering on living stone and how it would be perceived, which would make it scared of the harsh outside environment.

EDIT: Worn - A tale based on SCP-1503 by Captain Cain

Also some artwork by MonkeyClone: (LINK)

Putting this here for better exposure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the samely same vein, I'm not sure what I think of this, so I'm going to abstain from voting. It's not badly written… I just don't really get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the same vein, I just don't care for it. There's nothing exceptionally wrong with it, but nothing makes it stand out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dang, lotsa new skips tonight.

Anyways, hm. I'm not sure why I like this, I just do. Take a +1.
